content: The Higgs boson, also known as the "God particle," is a fundamental particle that is necessary to explain how other particles acquire mass. According to the Standard Model of particle physics, the Higgs field, which is associated with the Higgs boson, permeates all of space and gives mass to particles that interact with it. The existence of the Higgs boson was confirmed in 2012 by experiments at the Large Hadron Collider, and its discovery was a major milestone in the validation of the Standard Model.
